title: call_build_for_error: MaybeUninit::assume_init_ref() is UB by Rust rules
description: In tests, call_build_for_error takes &(SpendParameters, OutputParameters) but tests for early-return errors pass MaybeUninit::<(SpendParameters, OutputParameters)>::uninit().assume_init_ref(). The comment explains all nine tested error variants return before params is dereferenced. In practice no read occurs. However, Rust's reference rules state that creating a reference to uninitialized memory is immediate UB regardless of whether you read through it — the language permits the compiler to assume all references are valid and exploit that for optimization. This is technically unsound even if current rustc does not miscompile it. Suggested rewrite: split call_build_for_error into two separate helpers — one for pre-param errors that takes no params argument, and one for post-param errors that takes &Params. Alternatively, pass Option<&Params> and return BuilderError('params unavailable') when None — which is what the function already does for load_sapling_params failures.
